commands.repair_reason: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the branch already contains the narrow code change and regression coverage, leaving normal maintainer merge validation.; Cleared: The revised diff gates reply-policy elevation to authorized actual slash commands and does not touch dependencies, CI, secrets, or broader execution surfaces. commands.review_summary:
The PR carries iMessage control-command detection into inbound dispatch, sets `CommandSource="text"` only for authorized slash-command turns, and adds focused iMessage inbound-processing regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main sets iMessage command authorization without an explicit text command source, while reply delivery only auto-delivers explicit command turns. The discussion also provides a live packaged-Gateway before/after report for direct iMessage DM commands.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the branch already contains the narrow code change and regression coverage, leaving normal maintainer merge validation.

target.title: Fix iMessage slash command acknowledgements
target.body:
## Summary

Fix iMessage slash command acknowledgement delivery by marking authorized iMessage text commands with `CommandSource="text"` only when the inbound message is actually a control command.

## Root cause

The `/new` reset handler still returns its acknowledgement, but iMessage inbound turns only set `CommandAuthorized=true`. They did not set the matching text command source signal used by reply delivery policy. That left authorized slash commands eligible for direct-chat source reply suppression, so the reset completed while the acknowledgement was dropped.

The first version of this PR set `CommandSource` from `commandAuthorized` alone. That was too broad because authorization can be true for ordinary allowed iMessage messages. The updated patch carries the real control-command detection result into the dispatch decision and sets `CommandSource="text"` only when the sender is authorized and the inbound body is an actual text slash command.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Authorized iMessage text slash commands such as `/status`, `/new`, and `/restart` identify themselves as explicit text-sourced slash command turns, allowing command acknowledgements to follow the explicit command reply path.

Live packaged-Gateway proof: @flo246874 confirmed the bug class on OpenClaw 2026.5.18 with direct iMessage DM commands. Before the local dist patch, authorized `/status` was accepted and updated the direct iMessage session, but no native reply was delivered while normal non-command replies still worked. After gating source marking on actual authorized control commands, `/status` replied immediately, `/new` acknowledged successfully, and `/restart` restarted the Gateway and returned healthy with iMessage OK.

Local source-level verification from this PR head:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-imessage.config.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/inbound-processing.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/imessage/src/monitor/inbound-processing.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/inbound-processing.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
RUN  v4.1.6 /Users/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace-homer/tmp/openclaw-src

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  31 passed (31)
```

Observed result after fix: The focused iMessage inbound-processing suite verifies that an authorized `/new` iMessage DM produces `CommandAuthorized=true`, `CommandSource="text"`, and a `CommandTurn` matching `{ kind: "text-slash", source: "text", authorized: true, commandName: "new" }`. It also verifies that an authorized non-command iMessage DM keeps `CommandSource` undefined and normalizes as a regular message turn rather than a text-slash command turn.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-imessage.config.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/inbound-processing.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/imessage/src/monitor/inbound-processing.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/inbound-processing.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
